fault_hypothesis: The function incorrectly removes the last element from the merged list if both input lists are non-empty and end with the same value. The faulty action is the `merged.pop()` call on line 74.

constraints.intent: The total number of elements after the merge logic must equal the sum of elements from the inputs.

spec.expr: sum(1 for x in merged__1 if x == right_ids[-1]) == sum(1 for x in left_ids if x == right_ids[-1]) + sum(1 for x in right_ids if x == right_ids[-1])

constraints.intent: The count of the final element in the merged list must equal its total count from the inputs, even if it's the shared last element.
The merged list must contain all elements from both input lists before filtering.
The length of the returned list must be consistent with the input lengths when no stride is applied.
